scope.samplingNotes: Deep pass (v2). Shell and navigation from v1 preserved — no structural changes since 2026-04-04. Token layer audited line-by-line with exact hex values, CSS custom property counts, and TypeScript contract field counts. All 34 components/ui/ files individually inspected for hex bypasses (v1 reported 7 files, actual count is 20). Icon library imports recounted file-by-file. Page patterns sampled as in v1.

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Fixed top bar with search, project switcher, theme toggle. Shared across AppLayout and AgchainShellLayout.
Main app sidebar navigation with collapsible/compact mode, project switcher, workspace selector.
Section-specific secondary navigation rails for admin, AGChain, and flows detail surfaces.
shellOwnership.notes: Five different secondary nav components with no shared base, contract, or width token.
evidence: No shared SecondaryRail base component found

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Right panel container for help content and AI chat.
Four authenticated shell layouts each frame <main> differently.
shellOwnership.notes: AppLayout and AgchainShellLayout overlap significantly (both have header + left rail + optional right rail + content).

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Three public-facing layouts for marketing, login/register, and auth callbacks.
evidence: web/src/router.tsx assigns each public layout to specific route groups

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Primary token source. Dark-mode :root block: ~137 distinct CSS custom properties. Light-mode :root[data-theme='light'] block: ~68 overrides. @theme inline: 34 Tailwind utility mappings. Token groups: core semantic (20), sidebar (8), gray scale (9), status palette (5), shell layout (7), font sizes (6), spacing (4), workbench prose (13), decorative/accent (7), code pane (3), grid (5), overlays (3), JSON tree view (18), marketing (3), flow (5), left rail (4), admin config (11), radius (1), fonts (3). Primary brand color: #EB5E41 (same both modes).
evidence: web/src/tailwind.css:1-317 — full file read, values extracted line-by-line
Dark :root lines 5-177, light :root[data-theme='light'] lines 187-281, @theme inline lines 283-317

tokenInventory.driftNotes: 29 token pairs across 5 groups: SURFACE_TOKENS (7 pairs: background, chrome, card, secondary, muted, accent, popover), TEXT_TOKENS (4 pairs: foreground, muted-foreground, card-foreground, secondary-foreground), BORDER_TOKENS (3 pairs: border, input, sidebar-border), BRAND_TOKENS (4 pairs: primary, primary-foreground, ring, destructive), ADMIN_CONFIG_TOKENS (11 pairs: rail-bg, rail-border, frame-bg, header-bg, content-bg, 3 success status, 3 error status). Each pair has dark hex, light hex, cssVar reference, and note. Values match tailwind.css — this file is a TypeScript accessor, not an independent source.
evidence: SURFACE_TOKENS: 7 pairs, TEXT_TOKENS: 4, BORDER_TOKENS: 3, BRAND_TOKENS: 4, ADMIN_CONFIG_TOKENS: 11

tokenInventory.driftNotes: FONT_FAMILIES: 2 (Inter sans, JetBrains Mono mono). FONT_SIZES: 9-step scale (2xs 10px → 4xl 36px). FONT_WEIGHTS: 4 (normal 400, medium 500, semibold 600, bold 700). FONT_RECIPES: 11 usage recipes mapping contexts to exact Tailwind classes (page title → text-2xl font-bold, section heading → text-xl font-semibold, card title → text-sm font-semibold, body → text-sm font-normal, nav item → text-sm font-medium, label/caption → text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground, section label uppercase → text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground, badge → text-xs font-medium, data cell mono → text-xs, code/JSON mono → text-xs, dense grid cell → text-2xs).

ICON_SIZES: 6 (xs 14px, sm 16px, md 20px, lg 24px, xl 28px, xxl 32px). ICON_CONTEXT_SIZE: 5 semantic contexts (inline→sm, content→md, utility→md, nav→lg, hero→xl). ICON_STROKES: 3 (light 1.6, regular 1.8, strong 2.1). ICON_TONE_CLASS: 7 tones (current, default, muted, accent, success, warning, danger) mapped to Tailwind classes. ICON_STANDARD.migrationStatus: 'Hugeicons migration in progress'.

TOOLBAR_BUTTON: 10 properties (h-7, px-2, gap-1.5, text-xs, font-medium, leading-4, rounded-md, border, transition-colors, active:scale-[0.97]). TOOLBAR_BUTTON_STATES: 2 (active: border-border bg-background text-foreground; inactive: border-transparent text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent hover:text-foreground). TOOLBAR_STRIP: 6 properties (flex, gap-1, p-2, bg-card, border border-border rounded-md, gap-2). TOOLBAR_BUTTON_BASE: concatenated class string.

shell: 11 properties (headerHeight 60, headerHeightMobile 44, headerTallHeight 76, navbarWidth 220, navbarCompactWidth 60, navbarMobileWidth 200, navbarMinWidth 220, navbarMaxWidth 350, rightRailWidth 350, assistantWidth 360, contentMaxWidth via CSS var). grid: 2×5 raw hex colors (light/dark: background, chromeBackground, foreground, border, subtleText). admin: navWidth 200, shellTopBandHeight 40. Grid hex values duplicate CSS vars in tailwind.css.

tokenInventory.source: Hardcoded hex bypasses in UI primitives
tokenInventory.driftNotes: 20 of 34 UI primitive files contain hardcoded hex values in Tailwind classes. 7 distinct hex values, 80 total occurrences. Breakdown: #3a3a3a (31 occurrences, used as border — maps to --border), #e2503f (15 occurrences, used as focus ring — close to --primary #EB5E41 but not identical), #222221 (12 occurrences, used as hover bg — maps to --accent or --secondary), #f47a5c (10 occurrences, used as checked/selected text — lighter primary variant, no token equivalent), #31312e (5 occurrences, used as muted bg — between --secondary #1a1a1a and --border #2a2a2a), #111110 (3 occurrences, used as deep bg — near --background #0e0e0e), #55221e (4 occurrences, used as primary-tinted bg — no token equivalent). 15 files are clean: app-icon, badge, button, field, input, native-select, provider, scroll-area, separator, sheet, sidebar, skeleton, toolbar-button, segmented-control, badge.test.
evidence: Every .tsx file in web/src/components/ui/ individually inspected

componentContracts.usageNotes: 34 .tsx files (including 1 test). 22 Ark UI wrappers, 4 Radix UI wrappers, 8 standalone components. Ark UI (@ark-ui/react v5.32.0) is the primary primitive library. Radix UI provides dialog (@radix-ui/react-dialog), separator, tooltip, and slot. Standalone: AppIcon, Badge, Button, Input, NativeSelect, Skeleton, ToolbarButton, SegmentedControl.
evidence: 22 Ark UI wrappers, 4 Radix wrappers, 8 standalone

componentContracts.usageNotes: react-data-grid used in app surfaces (block viewer, settings sample). ag-grid-react used in marketing demo only.
Two competing components with different visual language. No shared EmptyState in components/ui/ or components/common/.
Single dismissible error alert. No competition.

pagePatterns.structureNotes: Data table with pagination, loading, toast feedback.
FlowDetail uses FlowsShellLayout with fixed sidebar rail + main content with tabs.
pagePatterns.shellAlignmentNotes: FlowDetail has its own shell layout.

pagePatterns.structureNotes: Locked 3-column Workbench with persistent pane scaffolds. PlanTracker uses 26/52/22 width ratio with locked layout, no drag, no toolbar. TestIntegrations uses similar pattern.
Two settings architectures: app settings under AppLayout, AGChain settings under AgchainShellLayout.
Self-contained under AgchainShellLayout with AgchainWorkspaceProvider context.

navigationStructure.routeMapping: web/src/router.tsx defines route tree with nested layout wrappers.
navigationStructure.breadcrumbConventions: AppBreadcrumbs component exists. Pages primarily use ShellPageHeader via HeaderCenterContext.
navigationStructure.actionPlacement: Primary actions in page headers via HeaderCenterContext right slot. Table row actions in dropdown menus. Toolbar actions use toolbar-contract.ts patterns.

statePresentation.loading: Mixed. AuthGuard and SuperuserGuard use inline spinners/text. Skeleton component exists in components/ui/skeleton.tsx but is not universally used.
statePresentation.empty: Fragmented. AgchainEmptyState and FlowEmptyState serve same role with different visual language. No shared foundation EmptyState.
statePresentation.error: ErrorAlert component exists in components/common/. Not universally used.
statePresentation.success: Consistent. Sonner toast library via UIProvider.
statePresentation.permission: Well-structured. AuthGuard + SuperuserGuard (three admin surface tiers).

accessibilityNotes: Focus ring color #e2503f is hardcoded in 12 UI primitive files instead of using --ring token (#EB5E41). The values are close but not identical.
20 of 34 UI primitive files use dark-mode-only hex values with no light mode equivalent.
Three icon libraries with different size conventions may produce inconsistent touch targets.

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Token system compliance across Ark UI wrapper components

competingImplementations.name: CSS custom property tokens (tailwind.css)
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Semantic surface, border, accent, and focus tokens with full light/dark coverage. --border: dark #2a2a2a / light #d6d3d1. --primary: #EB5E41 both modes. --ring: #EB5E41 both modes. --accent: dark #1a1a1a / light #f5ebe6.

competingImplementations.name: Hardcoded hex values in 20 UI primitive files
competingImplementations.location: 20 files in web/src/components/ui/ (accordion, checkbox, collapsible, combobox, dialog, file-upload, menu, number-input, pagination, popover, progress, select, segment-group, splitter, steps, switch, tabs, tags-input, tooltip, tree-view)
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Dark-mode-specific styling for Ark UI component wrappers. 7 distinct values: #3a3a3a (border, 31×), #e2503f (focus ring, 15×), #222221 (hover bg, 12×), #f47a5c (checked/selected text, 10×), #31312e (muted bg, 5×), #111110 (deep bg, 3×), #55221e (primary-tinted bg, 4×). Total: 80 occurrences.

evidence: 20 files with hex bypasses, 15 files clean
#3a3a3a (31×) vs --border dark value #2a2a2a — NOT the same value
#222221 (12×) vs --accent dark #1a1a1a — NOT the same value
#f47a5c (10×) — lighter primary variant, NO existing token equivalent
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: The Ark UI primitives use a parallel color palette that approximates but does not match the semantic tokens. 4 of the 7 hex values (#3a3a3a, #e2503f, #222221, #111110) are close to existing tokens but intentionally different values. 3 of the 7 (#f47a5c, #31312e, #55221e) have no token equivalent at all — they would need new tokens or mapping to existing ones. This is not simple find-and-replace; it requires deciding whether the Ark UI palette should converge to the existing tokens or whether the token system needs to expand to include these intermediate values.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Define 3-4 new semantic tokens for the Ark UI interaction states that have no current equivalent: a 'selected-text' token (for #f47a5c), an 'interactive-surface' token (for #31312e), and a 'primary-tinted-bg' token (for #55221e). Then migrate all 20 files to reference semantic tokens instead of hex. The close-but-not-identical values (#3a3a3a vs --border #2a2a2a, #e2503f vs --ring #EB5E41) should converge to the existing tokens unless there is a documented reason for the difference.
discussionQuestions: Is the Ark UI palette intentionally different from the semantic tokens (e.g., #3a3a3a is deliberately lighter than --border #2a2a2a), or is this accidental drift?
Should we add new semantic tokens for the 3 values with no equivalent (#f47a5c, #31312e, #55221e), or should these converge to existing tokens?
Should the focus ring be #e2503f (current Ark UI value) or #EB5E41 (--ring/--primary token value)?
Should the migration happen in one batch or file-by-file with visual verification?

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Canonical icon library and icon component contract

competingImplementations.name: Tabler Icons
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Legacy/primary icon library with widest usage. Top 5: IconPlus (8×), IconChevronRight (7×), IconDownload (6×), IconX (6×), IconLoader2 (5×).

competingImplementations.name: Hugeicons
competingImplementations.location: @hugeicons/react (15 files, generic HugeiconsIcon component only — no named icon imports)
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Migration target per icon-contract.ts. Used via dynamic component rendering, not named imports.

competingImplementations.name: Lucide React
competingImplementations.location: lucide-react (8 files, 13 distinct icons, 16 total imports)
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Used in UI primitives. Top icons: FileIcon (2×), UploadIcon (2×), XIcon (2×).

evidence: Tabler: 101 files, 48 distinct icons, 122 total imports
Hugeicons: 15 files — all use generic HugeiconsIcon component, zero named icon imports
Lucide: 8 files, 13 distinct icons, 16 total imports
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: Three libraries accumulated over time. Tabler dominates (101 files). Hugeicons is declared migration target but only 15 files use it, and only through the generic component — no named icon imports anywhere. Lucide came via shadcn/UI primitives.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Execute the documented Hugeicons migration. The AppIcon abstraction in icon-contract.ts should gate all future usage. Hugeicons' current 15-file footprint uses the generic HugeiconsIcon component pattern, which is the intended AppIcon approach.
discussionQuestions: Is the Hugeicons migration still the intended direction?
Should AppIcon be built before migration begins, or should migration proceed file-by-file?
What is the priority of icon migration vs other foundation work?

competingImplementations.name: ag-grid-react
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Feature-rich commercial grid for marketing demo.

conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: Two grid libraries serve different surfaces. ag-grid is marketing-only with a theme factory. react-data-grid is used in actual app surfaces without a theme factory.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Standardize on react-data-grid for app surfaces. Evaluate whether marketing can use react-data-grid to eliminate the ag-grid dependency.
discussionQuestions: Is ag-grid needed for any planned app features, or is it marketing-only?
Should react-data-grid get a theme factory?

competingImplementations.name: AdminLeftNav
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Left nav for 3 admin surfaces under AdminShellLayout.

competingImplementations.name: FlowsDetailRail
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Fixed sidebar for flow detail view.

evidence: 5 different secondary nav components with no shared base
No shared SecondaryRail, SectionNav, or DetailRail base component found
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: Each section built its own rail independently. No shared primitive was extracted.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Extract a shared SecondaryRail primitive that accepts a link config array. AdminLeftNav is the broadest user (3 admin surfaces).
discussionQuestions: Should all secondary rails share a single width token?
Should the secondary rail support collapse behavior?

competingImplementations.name: AgchainEmptyState
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Card with title, description, optional action and eyebrow.

competingImplementations.name: FlowEmptyState
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Gradient icon container with title and subtitle.

evidence: No shared EmptyState in components/ui/ or components/common/
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: Built independently with different visual language. Neither lives in a shared location.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Create shared EmptyState in components/common/ or components/ui/ with AgchainEmptyState's API. FlowEmptyState's gradient icon can be a variant.
discussionQuestions: Card style (AgchainEmptyState) or gradient icon style (FlowEmptyState)?
Should inline empty text also use the component?

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Authenticated shell layout for header + left-rail + content

competingImplementations.name: AppLayout
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Main app shell with TopCommandBar, LeftRailShadcn, RightRailShell.

competingImplementations.name: AgchainShellLayout
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: AGChain workspace shell with TopCommandBar, AgchainLeftNav — structurally mirrors AppLayout.

evidence: Both render TopCommandBar + resizable left sidebar + content
Both implement resizable left sidebar with localStorage persistence
AppLayout has right rail; AgchainShellLayout does not
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: AgchainShellLayout was built separately for AGChain's workspace context but its shell framing is nearly identical to AppLayout.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Evaluate whether AppLayout can accept left-rail and context-provider slots so AGChain uses it directly.
discussionQuestions: Should AppLayout become configurable with left-rail slots?
Should the right rail be available in AGChain workspace?

cleanAreas.summary: TopCommandBar is the single header component. HeaderCenterContext provides injection.
AuthGuard is the single auth wrapper.
useTheme hook with data-theme attribute. Single owner.
tailwind.css is the single source. ~137 dark-mode properties, ~68 light-mode overrides, 34 @theme inline mappings.
SuperuserGuard handles three admin surface tiers via shared AdminSurfaceGuard.
font-contract.ts: 2 families, 9 sizes, 4 weights, 11 recipes. Single source.
toolbar-contract.ts: 10 button properties, 2 states, 6 strip properties. Single source.
icon-contract.ts: 6 sizes (14-32px), 5 contexts, 3 strokes, 7 tones. Single source, even though the library is fragmented.
color-contract.ts: 29 token pairs across 5 groups. TypeScript accessor for tailwind.css values, not an independent source. Values match.
Three public layouts serve distinct route groups with no overlap.
